approach: Tolerate non-array task_summaries.concerns/files at three layers — normalize at the reader, guard in TasksCard, error-boundary around ActivityPanel — without changing the canonical TaskSummary type or forcing SDD writers to change shape.
last_action: PR #159 opened — fix(spec-viewer): tolerate non-array task_summaries concerns/files
decisions: Extracted toStringArray to its own .ts file (rather than keeping it module-local in TasksCard.tsx) so it can be imported by a non-JSX .test.ts file under the existing Jest config (testEnvironment: node, no jsx in tsconfig.test.json). Same helper logic; just lives one file over.

Skipped JSX render tests for TasksCard — Jest config has no jsdom and no preact-testing-library dep. The crash boundary is the toStringArray helper, so exercising it directly (plus the end-to-end reader normalization via the regression fixture) covers R001 + R006 without adding test infra.
Added a `specs/_*/` ignore rule for local-only demo spec dirs so the local reproducer (specs/_demo-string-concerns/) stays out of the PR.

T001.did: Wrote specs/095-fix-tasks-card-concerns/fixtures/legacy-string-concerns.spec-context.json — verbatim reconstruction of the final legacy fixture state with 4 task_summary entries whose concerns are plain strings.

T002.did: Added toStringArray defensive coercion in TasksCard.tsx and refactored both concerns and files render paths to use it.
concerns: Helper extracted to its own toStringArray.ts file (not kept module-local as the task said) so it can be tested by a .test.ts file under the existing Jest config.

T003.did: Extended normalizeSpecContext to coerce every task_summaries[*].concerns and .files; mutates only when at least one entry changes (NFR001).

T004.did: Replaced the two-line TASK SUMMARIES directive with explicit shape guidance: status DONE/DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, files string[], concerns string[], omit when empty.
concerns: Initial four-line version overshot the 2000-char preamble budget (got 2044). Trimmed to three lines preserving every shape assertion to stay under the limit — adjusted T009 to match the trimmed wording.

T005.did: Created ActivityErrorBoundary as a Preact class component that posts webviewError via vscode.postMessage and renders a quiet inline notice on catch.
concerns: Added 'webviewError' to ViewerToExtensionMessage in BOTH webview/src/spec-viewer/types.ts and src/features/spec-viewer/types.ts (these are duplicate definitions across the bundle boundary). Added a matching handler in messageHandlers.ts that logs to the output channel (NFR002).

T006.did: Wrapped <ActivityPanel/> in <ActivityErrorBoundary> in App.tsx — no layout change.

T007.did: Added 8 unit tests against toStringArray covering all R001 / R006 shapes including the regression fixture's strings.
concerns: Wrote a .test.ts (not .test.tsx) and exercised the helper directly rather than rendering the component — testEnvironment: 'node', no jsdom, and tsconfig.test.json has no jsx setting. The error-boundary force-throw assertion was not added since rendering the boundary needs the same JSX infra; the boundary's own logic is type-checked by tsc and trivially exercised by usage.

T008.did: Added 9 unit tests for normalizeSpecContext.task_summaries covering every R002 coercion rule plus a regression test that reads the regression fixture and asserts every concerns/files field is string[] (or omitted).

T009.did: Added a single test against the new directive's exact wording (DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, string[], Omit concerns when empty, never write "None"/"N/A") so future drift fails loudly.

FOLLOWUP.did: Per CP1 feedback: copied the fixture into specs/_demo-string-concerns/ (gitignored) for manual viewer reproduction, and added ActivityPanel.stories.tsx with 5 scenarios (empty, canonical, legacy-shape regression, mixed shapes, error boundary).

specify.key_finding: TasksCard at webview/src/spec-viewer/components/cards/TasksCard.tsx:74-80 calls .map() on task_summaries[*].concerns assuming string[], but the AI-injected prompt directive in src/ai-providers/promptBuilder.ts:43-44 doesn't specify the shape, so writers emit a plain string like "None" and crash the whole Activity panel.

plan.approach_summary: Tolerate non-array task_summaries.concerns/files at three layers — normalize at the reader, guard in TasksCard, error-boundary around ActivityPanel — without changing the canonical TaskSummary type or forcing SDD writers to change shape.
risks: Reader coercion changes data semantically when stripping "None" — mitigated by spec Out-of-Scope and verified-no-other-consumers grep.
Prompt drift between speckit-companion and SDD — handled by making the viewer tolerant of both shapes, not by coordinating the SDD repo.
